old_claim.note: Hospital Information Systems provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains from in_vitro_fertilization to hospital_information_systems; HIS is clinical administration infrastructure rather than part of IVF's laboratory and reproductive-medicine method.

source_shape.source_locator: Nobel 2010 popular-information description of the 1978 IVF birth, Bourn Hall clinic, and method spread.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source describes IVF through fertilized eggs, embryo transfer, clinical infertility treatment, and later clinical center development, without making hospital information systems part of the method.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This rejects a direct HIS prerequisite because the successful 1978 method and early IVF center predate modern digital hospital information-system dependency.

ontology_before: IVF included hospital information systems as a direct prerequisite, mixing a reproductive technology with later clinical data infrastructure.
ontology_after: IVF is modeled through culture, clinical medicine, and reproductive physiology; hospital information systems remain separate workflow infrastructure.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: hospital information systems no longer appear as a direct IVF prerequisite.
would_reject_if: The IVF node were re-scoped to a modern clinic-management workflow requiring HIS integration.
A source showed IVF laboratory fertilization and embryo transfer require a hospital information system.
Removing the edge also removed clinical medicine from IVF.
short_reason: Hospital information systems support care administration, not the IVF method.
